file: the operation-usage module, EXACTLY the 3 files the commit modified, in full: src/store/chat/slices/aiChat/actions/gatewayEventHandler.ts (672L) + .../heterogeneousAgentExecutor.ts (1531L) — the two store write-paths — + src/features/Conversation/ChatInput/OpStatusTray.tsx (consumer); plus src/utils/operationUsageMetrics.ts (unchanged context: the accumulate + derive primitives)

catch_key.problem: The operation's token-usage TOTAL is maintained as a parallel stored accumulator: the store write-paths accumulate a running total on each event and persist it, and the consumer PREFERS that stored total over deriving it from the per-message usage it already has — so two copies of one fact can drift apart.
catch_key.correct_direction: Make the per-message usage the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: DELETE the stored/accumulated total in the write-path(s) and DERIVE the total from the per-message usage in the consumer (so the tray always equals the sum of the bubbles). Removing the duplicate stored copy is the essence.
catch_key.inverted_direction: Treating the stored accumulator as the trusted total and recommending to KEEP/strengthen it while DROPPING the derive-from-messages path (or making the derivation defer to the stored value) — i.e. entrenching the duplicated source of truth instead of removing it.

fix_summary: The operation's token-usage total was kept as a PARALLEL ACCUMULATOR in the chat store: BOTH gatewayEventHandler.ts AND heterogeneousAgentExecutor.ts called addUsageToOperationMetrics on every step_complete/turn_metadata event and stored the running total via updateOperationMetadata; OpStatusTray then PREFERRED that stored total, deriving from per-message usage only as a fallback. The maintainer DELETED the stored accumulation in both handlers and made the tray derive the total from per-message usage — explicitly 'single source of truth', fixing drift between the tray and the per-bubble numbers. The util operationUsageMetrics.ts (unchanged) carries both the accumulate primitive and the derive fold, side by side. SCOPE: before.txt is the WHOLE module the maintainer touched, full files, UN-curated — the parallel-accumulation write-path (the actual sin) is in front of the reviewer amid ~2700 lines of real handler code, NOT excerpted to point at the answer. An earlier version omitted the store write-paths, so every trial missed the bug (it was off-screen); that data point was void. NOTE: provenance only — the grader scores against fix.diff.
